agent_reply: - NVS-backed 32-byte master key storage under namespace `aegis`, blob `master_key`
- in-RAM cached master key with explicit zeroization on `deinit()`
- `Aegis::rand()` using ESP hardware randomness mixed with uptime counters
- A normal firmware build was attempted earlier and still hits the existing `.iram0.text` overflow at link time, unrelated to Aegis.

- Since nothing includes `Aegis` yet, PlatformIO does not currently compile it as part of the dependency graph. I tried a direct compile using `compile_commands.json`, but Windows rejected the generated command because it was too long.
- NVS-backed 32-byte master key storage under namespace `aegis`, blob `master_key`
- in-RAM cached master key with explicit zeroization on `deinit()`
- `Aegis::rand()` using ESP hardware randomness mixed with uptime counters
- A normal firmware build was attempted earlier and still hits the existing `.iram0.text` overflow at link time, unrelated to Aegis.

- Since nothing includes `Aegis` yet, PlatformIO does not currently compile it as part of the dependency graph. I tried a direct compile using `compile_commands.json`, but Windows rejected the generated command because it was too long.

commit_message: more security related stuff, added aegis

user_prompt: I want to run `void test_sdcard()` to test performance of the recorder with encryption enabled
make sure everything is wired so that it works with encryption actually built and active
agent_reply: Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.

user_prompt: inside `lib\Security\Aegis.h`, provide a constant for a salt that will be used, use "98 c2 5a f2 b7 0f a4 b3 42 b4 64 e5 ee d6 ff 3d 0d d8 21 9c 9d 7b 16 b4 ce de cf fa ca 4e f3 2f " which I randomly generated, we'll use this for now. Also provide a constant for its length
agent_reply: Added `Aegis::kSalt` and `Aegis::kSaltSize` in [Aegis.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Security/Aegis.h:10). It’s a 32-byte `static constexpr uint8_t` array using your generated salt.

commit_message: added default salt and test password gen to aegis

user_prompt: we are reworking how security works, there are now two keys, filecrypt-key and network-key, I want the filecrypt key to have limited exposure.
first, fill in `kSaltNetwork` with "36 22 5c 86 c1 70 f2 c1 11 d3 d6 df 57 6e 28 93 71 4e 52 6c d8 43 b2 6b df ee 1f 12 c9 a3 e5 b2 "

then extend `Aegis::init` to load two keys into memory, `hasMasterKey` should not be renamed and reflect if both keys are available. But `getMasterKey` and `setMasterKey` and similar getters and setters now need to reflect the names `filecrypt` and `network`
`setTestTempMasterKey` also needs to be split into two and `setTestTempPassword` needs to create both keys
agent_reply: - `Aegis::init()` now loads both `filecrypt_key` and `network_key` from NVS.
- `hasMasterKey()` still exists, and now returns true only when both keys are available.
- `setTestTempPassword()` now derives both keys using separate salts.
- Audio recording encryption now uses only the filecrypt key.
- Web auth/config encryption/cloud upload auth now use the network key.
- Updated `test_sdcard()` to install deterministic test keys for both slots.
Also made `Aegis::init()` open NVS read-only and close the handle after loading; setters open write access only when actually saving keys.
Build passed. I restored `src/version.c` after PlatformIO regenerated it.
- `Aegis::init()` now loads both `filecrypt_key` and `network_key` from NVS.
- `hasMasterKey()` still exists, and now returns true only when both keys are available.
- `setTestTempPassword()` now derives both keys using separate salts.
- Audio recording encryption now uses only the filecrypt key.
- Web auth/config encryption/cloud upload auth now use the network key.
- Updated `test_sdcard()` to install deterministic test keys for both slots.
Also made `Aegis::init()` open NVS read-only and close the handle after loading; setters open write access only when actually saving keys.
Build passed. I restored `src/version.c` after PlatformIO regenerated it.

commit_message: I'm checking in this state before tearing it all down

user_prompt: are hashed together. The user is shown a part of that hash on the LCD screen. If the code is not what the user remembers, the user can suspect the device has been tampered with.
sorry for the interruption, I was in another window and stopped highlighting the thing I wanted you to see, let's try again

Add to `Aegis` a calculation for a tamper-evidence-code, as described by the text I am highlighting in the IDE. The hash can be just SHA1, have one call that returns the full result and another one that ends up being a uint32_t portion of it.

Also, there needs to be a cached hash of the network configuration, and "/set_cfg" needs to compute that hash again, to detect changes. Detecting a change should trigger the filecrypt-key to be regenerated.
agent_reply: - `Aegis` now has SHA1 support, cached network-config hashing, full tamper-evidence hash, and a `uint32_t` tamper-evidence code.
- `Aegis::generateFilecryptKey()` now generates a random 32-byte filecrypt key with `esp_fill_random()` and saves it to NVS.
- `WifiManager` refreshes the cached network-config hash on secured config load/save/default clear.
- `/set_cfg` now compares the previous and staged network config hashes, and regenerates the filecrypt key after a successful Wi-Fi config save when the network config changed.
git diff --check -- lib\Security\Aegis.h lib\Security\Aegis.cpp lib\WifiManager\WebCfgHandlers.cpp lib\WifiManager\WifiManager.cpp

Small heads-up: the working tree also shows `inc/web_assets.h`, `src/version.c`, and `lib/Security/security-policies-specifications.md` dirty. I didn’t edit the doc; the build regenerated the asset/version files.
- `Aegis` now has SHA1 support, cached network-config hashing, full tamper-evidence hash, and a `uint32_t` tamper-evidence code.
- `Aegis::generateFilecryptKey()` now generates a random 32-byte filecrypt key with `esp_fill_random()` and saves it to NVS.
- `WifiManager` refreshes the cached network-config hash on secured config load/save/default clear.
- `/set_cfg` now compares the previous and staged network config hashes, and regenerates the filecrypt key after a successful Wi-Fi config save when the network config changed.
git diff --check -- lib\Security\Aegis.h lib\Security\Aegis.cpp lib\WifiManager\WebCfgHandlers.cpp lib\WifiManager\WifiManager.cpp

Small heads-up: the working tree also shows `inc/web_assets.h`, `src/version.c`, and `lib/Security/security-policies-specifications.md` dirty. I didn’t edit the doc; the build regenerated the asset/version files.

commit_message: added security levels in docs, will implement later

user_prompt: `scripts\sectools.py` should have a function that can read a "*.key" file, and return 32 bytes, which is the encryption key
`scripts\sectools.py` should have a main function, using argparse, the user can specify a password, and generates a "*.key" file

By default the "*.key" is 32 bytes, raw binary. If a 64 byte file, or larger, is detected, then attempt to parse it as some sort of hexadecimal string, it could be many formats. The best bet is to remove all "0x", then remove all non alphanumeric characters.
Other file sizes should trigger an fatal error.

`scripts\thefly_audio_decoder.py` should be modified to have an optional specified `*.key` file. If the key is specified, the file MUST use decryption. Implement the decryption function in `scripts\sectools.py`. Feeding in an unencrypted file should result in an error if a key is also specified. If the key is not specified, assume unencrypted file.
The encryption implementation is seen in `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp` so use that as a guide.
agent_reply: - Accepts 64+ byte hex-ish files by stripping `0x` and non-alphanumeric chars, then parsing the first 64 hex chars.
- Fatal errors on other key file sizes.
- CLI derives a `.key` from `--password` using PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with the filecrypt salt from `Aegis.h`.
- Implements AES-GCM recording decryption for encrypted `.rec` packets.
- If `--key` is supplied, the decoder must decrypt first.
- Unencrypted input with `--key` will fail through encrypted packet size/authentication checks.
- If no key is supplied, behavior stays unencrypted as before.
Also ran small synthetic AES-GCM decrypt and key-parse round trips successfully. Note: `scripts/sectools.py` is showing as untracked in git, even though it existed in your workspace as the TODO file.
- Accepts 64+ byte hex-ish files by stripping `0x` and non-alphanumeric chars, then parsing the first 64 hex chars.
- Fatal errors on other key file sizes.
- CLI derives a `.key` from `--password` using PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with the filecrypt salt from `Aegis.h`.
- Implements AES-GCM recording decryption for encrypted `.rec` packets.
- If `--key` is supplied, the decoder must decrypt first.
- Unencrypted input with `--key` will fail through encrypted packet size/authentication checks.
- If no key is supplied, behavior stays unencrypted as before.
Also ran small synthetic AES-GCM decrypt and key-parse round trips successfully. Note: `scripts/sectools.py` is showing as untracked in git, even though it existed in your workspace as the TODO file.

user_prompt: add a web server handler for "/reset_password", and the javascript function `passreset_onsubmit` will be the one to submit information to it via ajax
under security-level 0, "/reset_password" replies with an appropriate code indicating that it is not implemented or something to that effect.
* the frontend takes the password and generates filecrypt-key and network-key using `network_salt` and `filecrypt_salt` (this seems missing, copy it from `kSaltFilecrypt`)
* the backend receives these and saves them to the NVS through `Aegis`
* the frontend takes the password and generates network-key using `network_salt`
* the backend receives it and saves it to the NVS through `Aegis`, filecrypt-key is regenerated randomly

also, while you are changing this part, the password entry field needs a second duplicate password entry for the user to confirm the password by typing it twice. Disallow the change if they don't match. Make it look nice, so instead of everything in one row, now there's like... three rows of elements? Use a hidden 4th row to pop up the error that the passwords don't match (make it handle other errors, we might add other validations).
agent_reply: - Added Aegis helpers to save both keys together, or save network key while regenerating filecrypt key randomly in [Aegis.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Security/Aegis.h) and [Aegis.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Security/Aegis.cpp).
- Added frontend PBKDF2 derivation for `network_key` and `filecrypt_key` using `network_salt` and `filecrypt_salt` in [script.js](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js).
- Added `passreset_onsubmit()` with password confirmation, mismatch/error display, AJAX submit, and session refresh.

- Reworked the password reset UI into a cleaner multi-row form with a hidden message row in [index.html](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/index.html) and [styles.css](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/styles.css).
- Security level `0`: endpoint returns `501 Not Implemented`.
- Security level `1`: frontend derives and sends both `network_key` and `filecrypt_key`; backend saves both through `Aegis`.
- Security level `2+`: frontend sends only `network_key`; backend saves it and randomly regenerates `filecrypt_key`.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1` and `pio run -e thefly_sec2` compile through the changed C++ but fail at final link with the existing IRAM overflow: `iram0_0_seg` overflowed by `2152 bytes`.
